title: Steel Structure Factory Inspection: QC Checklist 2026
description: Complete quality control checklist for inspecting steel structures before shipment. Covers material verification, weld testing, coating checks, and pre-assembly inspection.
content:
Importing a steel structure from overseas is a six-figure decision. The single most important thing you can do to protect that investment — beyond choosing a certified supplier — is a thorough factory inspection before your steel leaves China.

This guide covers what to check, when to inspect, and how to verify that your steel structure quality control standards are being met.

## Why Factory Inspection Matters

| Problem | On-site Consequence | Estimated Cost |
|---------|-------------------|---------------|
| Wrong steel grade | Structural failure risk, engineering re-review | $5,000–20,000 |
| Poor welds (cracks, porosity) | Rejection by local engineer, re-welding on site | $3,000–15,000 |
| Incorrect bolt hole positions | Components won't fit, on-site drilling delays | $2,000–8,000 |
| Inadequate coating thickness | Premature corrosion, repaint within 3–5 years | $10,000–30,000 |
| Wrong dimensions | Rework or re-order | $10,000–50,000+ |

A $1,500–2,500 third-party inspection catches these issues before shipping — delivering a 10× to 50× return on investment.

## When to Inspect: The 4-Stage QC Model

### Stage 1: Pre-Production Review (Week 1)
Verify shop drawings match your approved design, steel grade confirmed, WPS qualified, coating specification documented.

### Stage 2: In-Process Inspection (Weeks 2–3)
Check CNC cutting accuracy, weld quality on first batch, welder qualifications, fit-up accuracy.

### Stage 3: Pre-Shipment Inspection (Week 4–5)
The most critical stage. Cover material verification, dimensional inspection, welding quality, surface preparation and coating, pre-assembly, and marking/documentation.

### Stage 4: Container Loading Inspection
Verify correct components, proper bracing, clear marking, container seal recorded.

## Stage 3 Deep Dive: Pre-Shipment QC Checklist

### 1. Material Verification
- Steel grade confirmation via Mill Test Certificates (EN 10204 3.1)
- Material thickness via ultrasonic thickness gauge (±0.3mm)
- Heat number traceability — 100% match required

### 2. Dimensional Inspection
- Column length: ±2mm | Rafter length: ±3mm
- Beam straightness: L/1000, max 5mm
- Bolt hole diameter: +0.5mm of bolt diameter
- Inspect 100% of columns, 20% of other members

### 3. Welding Quality
- Visual (VT): 100% of welds — no cracks, undercut ≤ 0.5mm
- Ultrasonic Testing (UT): 10% of butt welds
- Magnetic Particle (MT): 5% of fillet welds
- Priority welds: column-to-base-plate, rafter apex, crane beam splices

### 4. Surface Preparation and Coating
- Blast cleanliness: SA 2.5 minimum per ISO 8501-1
- Surface profile: 50–75μm for epoxy systems
- Total DFT: ≥ specified value (typically 200–280μm)
- Coating continuity: holiday detector test

### 5. Pre-Assembly / Trial Fit
Assemble one representative bay to verify bolt hole alignment before shipping.

### 6. Marking and Documentation
Every component must carry piece mark, heat number, and weight. Collect: inspection report, MTRs, welder certificates, NDT reports, coating records.

## Who Should Inspect?

| Option | Cost | Best For |
|--------|------|----------|
| Buyer visits factory | $1,500–3,000 | First order, large projects |
| Third-party (SGS/BV/TÜV) | $800–2,500 | Most buyers |
| Local inspector | $400–800/day | Repeat orders |
| Supplier self-inspection + video | Free | Low-risk repeat orders |

## Red Flags: When to Reject a Shipment

Stop the shipment if you find: steel grade mismatch, systematic weld defects (>5% of tested), DFT below 60% of spec, bolt hole misalignment in pre-assembly, or supplier refuses inspection access.

## How OldTie Handles Quality Control

- **6 inspection checkpoints** — Incoming material → Cutting → Welding → Surface treatment → Pre-assembly → Final inspection
- **100% visual weld inspection** + 10% UT on critical welds
- **SA 2.5 blast standard** on every component
- **DFT verification** on every batch — recorded and shared
- **CE ISO certified steel structure** (EN 1090-2 EXC2, ISO 9001:2015)
- **5 production lines**, 5,000+ tons/month, Shangqiu, Henan

## Conclusion

Steel structure quality control is not about trust — it's about verification. A structured factory inspection catches issues that would cost 10–50× more to fix after delivery. For any project over $50,000, third-party pre-shipment inspection is not optional — it's essential.
